case.title: Batch Synthesis of 3,5-Disubstituted Isoxazole in TBAB/EG (1:5) DES
case.protocol: Phenylacetylene (1a, 0.5 mmol, 1.0 equiv) and ethyl nitroacetate (2, 1.0 mmol, 2.0 equiv) were added directly to TBAB/EG (1:5) DES (1 mL) in an oven-dried 30 mL vial equipped with a magnetic stirring bar; no additional solvent, catalyst, base, or additive was used. The reaction mixture was stirred at 120 °C in an oil bath for 15 min, at which point full conversion (99%) to ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate (3a) was achieved via 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition of the in-situ-generated nitrile oxide intermediate with phenylacetylene. After the reaction, the mixture was quenched with water, extracted with dichloromethane, dried over MgSO₄, and the solvent removed under reduced pressure; the isolated NMR yield was 83% (quantified against 1,3,5-trimethoxybenzene as internal standard).
case.notes: Local benchmark for vLLM Gemma upstream/council combinations against Claude.

streams.reasoning: From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: Reactants combined in DES solvent. Flow rate derived from total flow (0.5 mL/min) split equally.
From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: Reaction medium. Flow rate derived from total flow (0.5 mL/min) split equally.

pre_council_proposal.mixing_order_reasoning: From Chemistry Plan: Reagents are added directly to the DES medium. T-mixer provides sufficient initial contact for the viscous DES phase.
post_reactor_steps: Quench with Water: Necessary to stop reaction and facilitate extraction from DES as per Chemistry Plan.
pre_council_proposal.chemistry_notes: The reaction is a 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition involving the in-situ generation of a nitrile oxide. The use of TBAB/EG DES provides a polar, high-boiling environment. Design is driven by the high temperature (120°C) and the viscosity of the DES. Calculations indicate a laminar flow regime (Re=11) and a Damkohler number (Da=1.97) suggesting that mixing may be a limiting factor; however, the residence time of 15 min is sufficient to match batch conversion. A BPR is mandatory to prevent degassing and maintain phase stability at 120°C.

reasoning_per_field.residence_time_min: Calculation Step 2 estimated 15.0 min based on batch time (15 min) and analogy IF=1x. Analogy 2 used 20 min for a similar [3+2] cycloaddition, supporting this range.
reasoning_per_field.flow_rate_mL_min: Calculation Step 9: Total flow rate set to 0.5 mL/min to achieve the target residence time given the reactor volume.
reasoning_per_field.temperature_C: Maintained at 120°C as per Batch Protocol and Chemistry Plan to ensure rapid nitrile oxide generation and cycloaddition.
reasoning_per_field.concentration_M: Maintained at 0.5 M as per Batch Protocol and Calculation Step 1.
reasoning_per_field.BPR_bar: Calculation Step 8: BPR required (5.0 bar) to prevent degassing and maintain liquid phase at 120°C, as DES components and substrates may generate gas or have vapor pressure concerns.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_material: PFA selected for high temperature (120°C) stability and chemical compatibility with DES, as suggested by Thermal rules.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_ID_mm: Calculation Step 3: 1.0 mm ID used to ensure plug flow (Pe=173) and manage pressure drop (0.032 bar).
reasoning_per_field.reactor_volume_mL: Calculation Step 3: V_R = 7.5 mL (Residence time 15 min * Flow rate 0.5 mL/min).
reasoning_per_field.analogy_comparison: Analogy 1: Similar high-temp cyclization but different chemistry (energetic compound); used for BPR logic. Analogy 2: Chemically similar [3+2] dipolar cycloaddition; used to validate residence time (20 min vs our 15 min). Analogy 3: High-temp thermal cascade; used to validate PFA/Steel tubing for T > 100°C.

proposal.chemistry_notes:
The reaction is a 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition involving the in-situ generation of a nitrile oxide. The use of TBAB/EG DES provides a polar, high-boiling environment. Design is driven by the high temperature (120°C) and the viscosity of the DES. Calculations indicate a laminar flow regime (Re=11) and a Damkohler number (Da=1.97) suggesting that mixing may be a limiting factor; however, the residence time of 15 min is sufficient to match batch conversion. A BPR is mandatory to prevent degassing and maintain phase stability at 120°C.
Selected point is not engine-validated because the kinetic anchor was repaired/floored; run the screen experimentally before treating it as a final design.

reasoning_per_field.temperature_C: Maintained at 120°C as per Batch Protocol and Chemistry Plan to ensure rapid nitrile oxide generation and cycloaddition.
reasoning_per_field.concentration_M: Maintained at 0.5 M as per Batch Protocol and Calculation Step 1.
reasoning_per_field.BPR_bar: Calculation Step 8: BPR required (5.0 bar) to prevent degassing and maintain liquid phase at 120°C, as DES components and substrates may generate gas or have vapor pressure concerns.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_material: PFA selected for high temperature (120°C) stability and chemical compatibility with DES, as suggested by Thermal rules.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_ID_mm: Calculation Step 3: 1.0 mm ID used to ensure plug flow (Pe=173) and manage pressure drop (0.032 bar).
reasoning_per_field.reactor_volume_mL: Calculation Step 3: V_R = 7.5 mL (Residence time 15 min * Flow rate 0.5 mL/min).
reasoning_per_field.analogy_comparison: Analogy 1: Similar high-temp cyclization but different chemistry (energetic compound); used for BPR logic. Analogy 2: Chemically similar [3+2] dipolar cycloaddition; used to validate residence time (20 min vs our 15 min). Analogy 3: High-temp thermal cascade; used to validate PFA/Steel tubing for T > 100°C.

intensification_mandate.flow_justification_basis: Flow must demonstrate 6.0x residence-time reduction or a clear hazardous_intermediate advantage based on hazardous-intermediate holdup reduction.

council_messages.concern: The estimated conversion X=0.12 is low; the actual reaction rate in DES may differ from the model.

validation_experiments: Long-term stability run (4 hours) with pure DES at 120°C to monitor for pressure drift or precipitation
Step-wise temperature ramp (80°C to 140°C) to determine the onset of DES thermal decomposition
Pressure drop verification across the 19m PFA coil using the actual DES mixture to validate the 1.711 bar estimate

uncertainty_reasons: sub-unity analogy IF values were floored to 1.0
analogy IF is batch-equivalent while class IF predicts intensification
feasibility_diagnostic.diagnosis: Current kinetic anchor is uncertain, not a hard infeasibility: current kinetics require tau=15.0 min, but the intensification mandate limits tau to <= 2.5 min (6.0x faster than 15.0 min batch).
recommended_next_steps: Generate intensified screen candidates, but do not mark the selected point as engine-validated without experimental support.
Run a small flow screen to measure conversion at sub-batch tau values.
Test intensification levers: higher temperature, lower viscosity/dilution strategy, smaller ID or enhanced mixing, and staged/recycle operation if chemically justified.
Revisit analogy/kinetic anchor if it came from weak local-model reasoning rather than measured flow precedent.

final_design_candidate.human_explanation:
### 1. Proposed Flow Setup
*   **Reactor:** PFA coil (0.75 mm ID, 8.394 mL volume).
*   **Temperature:** 120.0 °C.
*   **Pressure:** 5.0 bar (via BPR).
*   **Residence Time:** 2.0 min.
*   **Total Flow Rate:** 4.197 mL/min.
*   **Concentration:** 0.5 M.
*   **Feed Streams:**
    *   **Stream A:** Phenylacetylene (0.5 M) and Ethyl nitroacetate (1.0 M) in TBAB/EG (1:5) Deep Eutectic Solvent (DES). Flow rate: 2.0985 mL/min.
    *   **Stream B:** Pure TBAB/EG (1:5) DES. Flow rate: 2.0985 mL/min.
*   **Mixing:** T-mixer.
*   **Post-Processing:** Immediate quench with water to stop the reaction and facilitate extraction from the DES phase.

### 2. Design Rationale
The design targets a 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition via an in-situ generated nitrile oxide. 
*   **Solvent:** TBAB/EG DES was chosen for its high boiling point and polarity, providing a stable environment for high-temperature operation.
*   **Temperature/Pressure:** 120 °C is required for rapid nitrile oxide generation. A BPR of 5.0 bar is mandatory to prevent degassing and maintain the liquid phase of the substrates and DES.
*   **Materials:** PFA tubing was selected for its chemical compatibility with DES and thermal stability at 120 °C.
*   **Analogies:** The residence time and BPR logic are based on similar [3+2] dipolar cycloadditions and high-temperature thermal cascades (referencing general high-temp cyclization and thermal cascade precedents).

### 3. Key Differences from Batch
*   **Residence Time:** Significant reduction from the 15-minute batch protocol to 2.0 minutes in flow.
*   **Mixing:** Transition from bulk stirring to a T-mixer/plug-flow regime.
*   **Thermal Control:** Improved heat transfer via the PFA coil compared to batch heating, reducing the risk of local hotspots.
*   **Intermediate Handling:** Reduced holdup of the reactive nitrile oxide intermediate.

### 4. Engineering Warnings & Flags
*   **Kinetic Uncertainty:** The design is a **screening hypothesis**. Engineering validation indicates that the predicted conversion at 2.0 min may be low ($\approx 12\%$). A residence time study (2–10 min) is strongly recommended to find the actual conversion curve.
*   **Mixing Limitations:** The system operates in a laminar flow regime ($\text{Re} \approx 11$). Mixing may be the rate-limiting step ($\text{Da} > 1$).
*   **Clogging Risk:** High viscosity of the DES increases the risk of precipitation or clogging. Heat-traced feed lines are recommended.
*   **Stability:** A 4-hour stability run with pure DES at 120 °C is advised to monitor for pressure drift or thermal decomposition.

**Confidence: LOW** — The closest literature analogy has a similarity score of 0.34 (threshold for MEDIUM: 0.50). No close precedent found in the corpus for this exact reaction class and conditions. Treat this proposal as a starting hypothesis requiring careful experimental validation before scale-up.

rounds.chain_of_thought:
### Stage 0 — Problem Framing

**Reaction class:** other  
**Flags:** thermal  
**Flow justified:** Yes

### Stage 1 — Candidate Matrix

**Strategy:** This is a thermal cycloaddition with a relatively long tau_center. To meet the intensification mandate of 6x reduction, the sampling must aggressively target the lower tau range while maintaining a broad enough window to anchor against the literature value.  
**Candidates to council:** 6  
**Hard-gate flagged (proceed with warnings):** 6

**Hard-gate flag details** *(candidates still evaluated — flags inform scoring agents)*:
- ⛔ Candidate 1 (τ=2.0 min, d=0.75 mm): dual-criterion mixing fail: Da_mass=1.44 > 1.0 AND r_mix=1.172 > 0.20
- ⛔ Candidate 2 (τ=2.0 min, d=1.0 mm): dual-criterion mixing fail: Da_mass=2.56 > 1.0 AND r_mix=2.083 > 0.20
- ⛔ Candidate 3 (τ=2.0 min, d=1.6 mm): dual-criterion mixing fail: Da_mass=6.55 > 1.0 AND r_mix=5.333 > 0.20
- ⛔ Candidate 4 (τ=2.2 min, d=0.75 mm): dual-criterion mixing fail: Da_mass=1.44 > 1.0 AND r_mix=1.065 > 0.20
- ⛔ Candidate 5 (τ=2.2 min, d=1.0 mm): dual-criterion mixing fail: Da_mass=2.56 > 1.0 AND r_mix=1.894 > 0.20
- ⛔ Candidate 6 (τ=2.2 min, d=1.6 mm): dual-criterion mixing fail: Da_mass=6.55 > 1.0 AND r_mix=4.848 > 0.20

**Preliminary design envelope:** τ ∈ [2.0, 2.2] min | d ∈ [0.75, 1.0, 1.6] mm | Q ∈ [3.81543, 12.06372] mL/min

The flow regime is laminar (Re=160), failing the 'slug_flow' requirement of the intensification mandate.

rounds.chain_of_thought:
### Selection Rationale

Candidate 1 is the winner with a combined score of 0.873, followed by Candidate 2 (0.829) and Candidate 3 (0.764). The winner's lead is driven by the highest domain score (0.955), specifically excelling in chemistry and kinetics adequacy. Candidate 2 was held back by a lower geometry score and slightly lower PVS. Candidate 3 was significantly penalized in the chemistry domain (0.450), likely due to mixing regime failures at the larger diameter. The 'balanced' objective favored the most robust chemical performance over raw productivity. The primary remaining uncertainty is the actual conversion (X=0.12) at the target tau, which appears low for a cycloaddition. The first bench experiment should verify the conversion at tau=2.0 min to validate the kinetics model.

### Weighted Score Comparison

| id | combined | chem | kin | fluid | safety | geo | boosted |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **1 ★** | **0.873** | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 0.700 | chemistry |
| **2** | **0.829** | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 0.638 | chemistry |
| **3** | **0.764** | 0.450 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 0.757 | chemistry |

### Resolved Trade-offs

- id=1 vs id=2: id=1 has higher combined score (0.873 vs 0.829) and better geometry score (0.700 vs 0.638).
- id=1 vs id=3: id=1 is vastly superior in chemistry domain (1.000 vs 0.450), making id=3 unsuitable despite higher productivity.

### Remaining Uncertainties

- The estimated conversion X=0.12 is low; the actual reaction rate in DES may differ from the model.

### Pump Flowrate Derivation

| Stream | Role | Q (mL/min) | Derivation |
|---|---|---|---|
| A | substrate | **0.0666** | ṅ_lim=0.0333 mmol/min × eq=1.0 / C_feed=0.5 M = 0.0666 mL/min |
| B | reagent | **0.0666** | ṅ_lim=0.0333 mmol/min × eq=1.0 / C_feed=0.5 M = 0.0666 mL/min |

ṅ_limiting = **0.0333 mmol/min** | Q_total = **0.1332 mL/min**

### Recommended First Experiment

Run a residence time study from 2 to 10 minutes to determine the actual conversion curve and verify if tau=2.0 is sufficient for the target yield.

### DFMEA — Failure Mode Analysis

| Rank | Mode | Cause | Severity | Likelihood | RPN | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Precipitation/Clogging of DES | High viscosity of TBAB/EG DES at lower temperatures or local concentration gradients leading to salt precipitation | 9 | 3 | **27** | Implement heat-traced feed lines and a pre-heating coil before the reactor zone |
| 2 | Thermal degradation of DES/Reactants | Local hotspots at 120°C due to low Re (119) and poor radial heat transfer in 0.75mm PFA tubing | 6 | 4 | **24** | Use a high-thermal-conductivity oil bath and monitor outlet temperature for degradation products |
| 3 | BPR Leakage/Failure | High viscosity of DES causing erratic pressure spikes or seal wear in the Back Pressure Regulator | 7 | 3 | **21** | Install a pressure transducer immediately upstream of the BPR to detect oscillations |

**Single points of failure:**
- BPR valve — blockage or seal failure leads to immediate loss of pressure and solvent flashing

**Validation experiments:**
1. Long-term stability run (4 hours) with pure DES at 120°C to monitor for pressure drift or precipitation
1. Step-wise temperature ramp (80°C to 140°C) to determine the onset of DES thermal decomposition
1. Pressure drop verification across the 19m PFA coil using the actual DES mixture to validate the 1.711 bar estimate
concerns: The estimated conversion X=0.12 is low; the actual reaction rate in DES may differ from the model.

### Pre-selection Expert Refinement

| id | combined | PVS | chemistry | kinetics | fluidics | safety | geometry | disq |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | 0.873 | 0.845 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 0.700 |  |
| 2 | 0.829 | 0.770 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 0.638 |  |
| 3 | 0.764 | 0.697 | 0.450 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 0.757 |  |

### Skeptic Audit
AUDIT: 2 issues found (0 CRITICAL, 0 HIGH, 2 scope violations). 0 candidates flagged for disqualification. No CRITICAL errors — council may proceed with caution.

### Winner — candidate id=1
- τ=2.0 min, d=0.75 mm, Q=4.19697 mL/min, V_R=8.3939 mL, L=19.0 m
- Re=119, ΔP=1.711 bar, r_mix=1.172, X=0.12, prod=3929.2 mg/h
- **Rationale**: Candidate 1 is the winner with a combined score of 0.873, followed by Candidate 2 (0.829) and Candidate 3 (0.764). The winner's lead is driven by the highest domain score (0.955), specifically excelling in chemistry and kinetics adequacy. Candidate 2 was held back by a lower geometry score and slightly lower PVS. Candidate 3 was significantly penalized in the chemistry domain (0.450), likely due to mixing regime failures at the larger diameter. The 'balanced' objective favored the most robust chemical performance over raw productivity. The primary remaining uncertainty is the actual conversion (X=0.12) at the target tau, which appears low for a cycloaddition. The first bench experiment should verify the conversion at tau=2.0 min to validate the kinetics model.
- **Flow-value justification**: Selected because it achieves the 6.0x residence-time reduction mandate while maintaining the highest combined score (0.873) and superior domain adequacy in chemistry and geometry compared to higher-throughput options. Warning: no concrete flow advantage identified. This design may not justify flow over batch.
- **Remaining uncertainties**:
  - The estimated conversion X=0.12 is low; the actual reaction rate in DES may differ from the model.

### DFMEA — Top Failure Modes
- [1] Precipitation/Clogging of DES: severity=9, likelihood=3, RPN=27 | mitigation: Implement heat-traced feed lines and a pre-heating coil before the reactor zone
- [2] Thermal degradation of DES/Reactants: severity=6, likelihood=4, RPN=24 | mitigation: Use a high-thermal-conductivity oil bath and monitor outlet temperature for degradation products
- [3] BPR Leakage/Failure: severity=7, likelihood=3, RPN=21 | mitigation: Install a pressure transducer immediately upstream of the BPR to detect oscillations

### Validation Experiments
- Long-term stability run (4 hours) with pure DES at 120°C to monitor for pressure drift or precipitation
- Step-wise temperature ramp (80°C to 140°C) to determine the onset of DES thermal decomposition
- Pressure drop verification across the 19m PFA coil using the actual DES mixture to validate the 1.711 bar estimate

proposal.mixing_order_reasoning: From Chemistry Plan: Reagents are added directly to the DES medium. T-mixer provides sufficient initial contact for the viscous DES phase.
post_reactor_steps: Quench with Water: Necessary to stop reaction and facilitate extraction from DES as per Chemistry Plan.
proposal.chemistry_notes:
The reaction is a 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition involving the in-situ generation of a nitrile oxide. The use of TBAB/EG DES provides a polar, high-boiling environment. Design is driven by the high temperature (120°C) and the viscosity of the DES. Calculations indicate a laminar flow regime (Re=11) and a Damkohler number (Da=1.97) suggesting that mixing may be a limiting factor; however, the residence time of 15 min is sufficient to match batch conversion. A BPR is mandatory to prevent degassing and maintain phase stability at 120°C.
Selected point is not engine-validated because the kinetic anchor was repaired/floored; run the screen experimentally before treating it as a final design.

reasoning_per_field.residence_time_min: Calculation Step 2 estimated 15.0 min based on batch time (15 min) and analogy IF=1x. Analogy 2 used 20 min for a similar [3+2] cycloaddition, supporting this range.
reasoning_per_field.flow_rate_mL_min: Calculation Step 9: Total flow rate set to 0.5 mL/min to achieve the target residence time given the reactor volume.
reasoning_per_field.temperature_C: Maintained at 120°C as per Batch Protocol and Chemistry Plan to ensure rapid nitrile oxide generation and cycloaddition.
reasoning_per_field.concentration_M: Maintained at 0.5 M as per Batch Protocol and Calculation Step 1.
reasoning_per_field.BPR_bar: Calculation Step 8: BPR required (5.0 bar) to prevent degassing and maintain liquid phase at 120°C, as DES components and substrates may generate gas or have vapor pressure concerns.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_material: PFA selected for high temperature (120°C) stability and chemical compatibility with DES, as suggested by Thermal rules.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_ID_mm: Calculation Step 3: 1.0 mm ID used to ensure plug flow (Pe=173) and manage pressure drop (0.032 bar).
reasoning_per_field.reactor_volume_mL: Calculation Step 3: V_R = 7.5 mL (Residence time 15 min * Flow rate 0.5 mL/min).
reasoning_per_field.analogy_comparison: Analogy 1: Similar high-temp cyclization but different chemistry (energetic compound); used for BPR logic. Analogy 2: Chemically similar [3+2] dipolar cycloaddition; used to validate residence time (20 min vs our 15 min). Analogy 3: High-temp thermal cascade; used to validate PFA/Steel tubing for T > 100°C.

safety_flags: SCREEN_REQUIRED: kinetic anchor uncertain; selected point is a screening hypothesis

validation_experiments: Long-term stability run (4 hours) with pure DES at 120°C to monitor for pressure drift or precipitation
Step-wise temperature ramp (80°C to 140°C) to determine the onset of DES thermal decomposition
Pressure drop verification across the 19m PFA coil using the actual DES mixture to validate the 1.711 bar estimate

uncertainty_reasons: sub-unity analogy IF values were floored to 1.0
analogy IF is batch-equivalent while class IF predicts intensification
feasibility_diagnostic.diagnosis: Current kinetic anchor is uncertain, not a hard infeasibility: current kinetics require tau=15.0 min, but the intensification mandate limits tau to <= 2.5 min (6.0x faster than 15.0 min batch).
recommended_next_steps: Generate intensified screen candidates, but do not mark the selected point as engine-validated without experimental support.
Run a small flow screen to measure conversion at sub-batch tau values.
Test intensification levers: higher temperature, lower viscosity/dilution strategy, smaller ID or enhanced mixing, and staged/recycle operation if chemically justified.
Revisit analogy/kinetic anchor if it came from weak local-model reasoning rather than measured flow precedent.

formatted_result.explanation:
### 1. Proposed Flow Setup
*   **Reactor:** PFA coil (0.75 mm ID, 8.394 mL volume).
*   **Temperature:** 120.0 °C.
*   **Pressure:** 5.0 bar (via BPR).
*   **Residence Time:** 2.0 min.
*   **Total Flow Rate:** 4.197 mL/min.
*   **Concentration:** 0.5 M.
*   **Feed Streams:**
    *   **Stream A:** Phenylacetylene (0.5 M) and Ethyl nitroacetate (1.0 M) in TBAB/EG (1:5) Deep Eutectic Solvent (DES). Flow rate: 2.0985 mL/min.
    *   **Stream B:** Pure TBAB/EG (1:5) DES. Flow rate: 2.0985 mL/min.
*   **Mixing:** T-mixer.
*   **Post-Processing:** Immediate quench with water to stop the reaction and facilitate extraction from the DES phase.

### 2. Design Rationale
The design targets a 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition via an in-situ generated nitrile oxide. 
*   **Solvent:** TBAB/EG DES was chosen for its high boiling point and polarity, providing a stable environment for high-temperature operation.
*   **Temperature/Pressure:** 120 °C is required for rapid nitrile oxide generation. A BPR of 5.0 bar is mandatory to prevent degassing and maintain the liquid phase of the substrates and DES.
*   **Materials:** PFA tubing was selected for its chemical compatibility with DES and thermal stability at 120 °C.
*   **Analogies:** The residence time and BPR logic are based on similar [3+2] dipolar cycloadditions and high-temperature thermal cascades (referencing general high-temp cyclization and thermal cascade precedents).

### 3. Key Differences from Batch
*   **Residence Time:** Significant reduction from the 15-minute batch protocol to 2.0 minutes in flow.
*   **Mixing:** Transition from bulk stirring to a T-mixer/plug-flow regime.
*   **Thermal Control:** Improved heat transfer via the PFA coil compared to batch heating, reducing the risk of local hotspots.
*   **Intermediate Handling:** Reduced holdup of the reactive nitrile oxide intermediate.

### 4. Engineering Warnings & Flags
*   **Kinetic Uncertainty:** The design is a **screening hypothesis**. Engineering validation indicates that the predicted conversion at 2.0 min may be low ($\approx 12\%$). A residence time study (2–10 min) is strongly recommended to find the actual conversion curve.
*   **Mixing Limitations:** The system operates in a laminar flow regime ($\text{Re} \approx 11$). Mixing may be the rate-limiting step ($\text{Da} > 1$).
*   **Clogging Risk:** High viscosity of the DES increases the risk of precipitation or clogging. Heat-traced feed lines are recommended.
*   **Stability:** A 4-hour stability run with pure DES at 120 °C is advised to monitor for pressure drift or thermal decomposition.

**Confidence: LOW** — The closest literature analogy has a similarity score of 0.34 (threshold for MEDIUM: 0.50). No close precedent found in the corpus for this exact reaction class and conditions. Treat this proposal as a starting hypothesis requiring careful experimental validation before scale-up.

case.title: Batch Synthesis of 3,5-Disubstituted Isoxazole in TBAB/EG (1:5) DES
case.protocol: Phenylacetylene (1a, 0.5 mmol, 1.0 equiv) and ethyl nitroacetate (2, 1.0 mmol, 2.0 equiv) were added directly to TBAB/EG (1:5) DES (1 mL) in an oven-dried 30 mL vial equipped with a magnetic stirring bar; no additional solvent, catalyst, base, or additive was used. The reaction mixture was stirred at 120 °C in an oil bath for 15 min, at which point full conversion (99%) to ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate (3a) was achieved via 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition of the in-situ-generated nitrile oxide intermediate with phenylacetylene. After the reaction, the mixture was quenched with water, extracted with dichloromethane, dried over MgSO₄, and the solvent removed under reduced pressure; the isolated NMR yield was 83% (quantified against 1,3,5-trimethoxybenzene as internal standard).
case.notes: Local benchmark for vLLM Gemma upstream/council combinations against Claude.

batch_record.reaction_description: 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition of the in-situ-generated nitrile oxide intermediate with phenylacetylene to produce ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
batch_record.raw_text: Phenylacetylene (1a, 0.5 mmol, 1.0 equiv) and ethyl nitroacetate (2, 1.0 mmol, 2.0 equiv) were added directly to TBAB/EG (1:5) DES (1 mL) in an oven-dried 30 mL vial equipped with a magnetic stirring bar; no additional solvent, catalyst, base, or additive was used. The reaction mixture was stirred at 120 °C in an oil bath for 15 min, at which point full conversion (99%) to ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate (3a) was achieved via 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition of the in-situ-generated nitrile oxide intermediate with phenylacetylene. After the reaction, the mixture was quenched with water, extracted with dichloromethane, dried over MgSO₄, and the solvent removed under reduced pressure; the isolated NMR yield was 83% (quantified against 1,3,5-trimethoxybenzene as internal standard).

chemistry_plan.quench_reasoning: Necessary to stop reaction and facilitate extraction from DES
chemistry_plan.confidence_notes: Direct translation from batch record; DES viscosity may be a flow consideration.

intensification_mandate.flow_justification_basis: Flow must demonstrate 6.0x residence-time reduction or a clear hazardous_intermediate advantage based on hazardous-intermediate holdup reduction.

analogies.record_id: Yang 등 - 2024 - Safety Guided Process Development and Scale-Up of the Highly Energetic Compound 4-Methyl-1,2,5-oxadi.pdf
analogies.summary: This process involves the synthesis of 4-methyl-1,2,5-oxadiazole, a highly energetic compound, using a nitration or cyclization reaction in a concentrated sulfuric acid/water mixture (63:6.3 ratio). The flow process was conducted in a tubular reactor at 154°C with a residence time of 179 minutes (approximately 3 hours) and a flow rate of 0.12 mL/min, requiring a back-pressure regulator at 47.6 bar to maintain liquid phase conditions. Flow chemistry enabled a significant temperature increase from 65°C in batch to 154°C, resulting in an improved yield from 70.8% to 78.8%. The primary advantage of flow over batch was enhanced safety when handling this highly energetic compound, as the continuous process allows for better control of the exothermic reaction and reduces the inventory of hazardous material at any given time. The high pressure and temperature conditions achievable in the flow reactor also contributed to the improved reaction efficiency.

additives.name: N-methyl morpholine
additives.role: forms stable NMM salt of the product

optimization_table.key_findings: Increasing the residence time to 180 min and temperature to 157 °C resulted in complete conversion and >99% product purity.

analogies.summary: This process demonstrates a [3 + 2] dipolar cycloaddition reaction conducted in a continuous flow unit (CFU) with automated pumps. The reaction uses DMSO as solvent with diisopropylamine as base, operating at 60°C with a 20-minute residence time at 0.165 mL/min flow rate, achieving 96% yield. The flow process allows for higher temperature operation (60°C vs 20°C in batch) while maintaining excellent yield, with the batch baseline achieving 100% yield at room temperature in the same reaction time. The continuous flow setup enables better temperature control and process intensification, allowing the reaction to proceed efficiently at elevated temperature with only a minimal decrease in yield compared to the batch process.

chemistry.scope_summary: The process shows a wide scope for synthesizing 1-aryl-1,2,3-triazoles from various anilines, including electron-rich and electron-poor substrates, yielding triazoles efficiently in good yields.

optimization_table.key_findings: CuI (10 mol%) with 1,10-phenanthroline (10 mol%) at 60°C for 20 min gave 100% conversion.

full_record.notes: Flow process achieves high yield with optimized conditions, BPR inferred from reactor scheme.

analogies.summary: This photocycloaddition reaction utilizes triplet sensitization with 2,2'-dimethoxythioxanthone (2,2'-MeOTX) as the photocatalyst at 1 mol% loading under 455 nm COB LED irradiation. The flow process operates in acetonitrile at 200°C with a 5-minute residence time and 6.0 mL/min flow rate using a FlowSyn steel coil reactor (30 mL volume) equipped with a 34 bar back-pressure regulator. Flow chemistry enabled daisy-chaining of the initial photochemical step with high-temperature thermal cascade reactions, dramatically improving yield from 16% in batch (2 hours) to 88% in flow with a 24-fold intensification factor. The high-temperature operation required for the thermal cascade was much safer to implement in the continuous flow reactor compared to batch processing.

chemistry.reaction_name: [2 + 2] photocycloaddition followed by thermal electrocyclic cascade
chemistry.bond_formed: C-C bonds in cyclobutane ring and subsequent cascade products
chemistry.scope_summary: Five aryl enamide derivatives tested (phenyl, 3-F, 3-Br, 3-OMe, 4-CF3) with yields of 63-68% for most substrates. The 4-CF3 derivative was resistant to the thermal fragmentation step.

optimization_table.key_findings: MeCN solvent with 0.5 M concentration at 200°C with 5 min residence time gave optimal results

figures.key_information: photochemical reactor setup with dual light sources positioned above a round bottom flask on a heating plate

The ease at which this could be purified by filtration represents one of the main benefits of acetonitrile over xylene, which would not be possible without the use of continuous flow technology. This result represents a 24 h productivity of 490 g. translation_logic.batch_limitation: Low yield (16%) and long reaction time (120 min) in batch mode
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Daisy-chaining photochemistry with high-temperature thermal cascade reactions enabled by flow technology, achieving high productivity
translation_logic.what_changed: Temperature increased to 200°C, residence time reduced to 5 min, back pressure of 34 bar applied
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Solvent (MeCN), concentration (0.5 M), light source power (36 W)
translation_logic.safety_improvement: High temperature thermal cascade reactions safer in flow than batch

figure_descriptions: Reactor scheme showing photochemical setup with light sources
Flow schematic for daisy-chained photo/thermal process

full_record.notes: High confidence extraction with clear quantitative data from optimization tables. Flow process shows dramatic improvement in yield (16% to 88%) and time reduction (120 min to 5 min). Productivity of 20 g/h mentioned in key claim.

steps.name: Batch Conditions
assumptions: Intensification policy active downstream → tau_flow must remain ≤ 15.0 min

steps.name: Kinetics & Residence Time
steps.summary: τ = 15.0 min, range [1.0–22.5] min, via analogy (IF = 1×)
warnings: One or more analogy IF values were below 1.0 and were floored to 1.0 to prevent extracted literature records from de-intensifying the flow design.
Methods disagree: τ_analogy = 15.0 min vs τ_class = 1.5 min (10.0× difference). Experimental verification strongly recommended.

steps.name: Reactor Sizing
steps.summary: V_R = 7.50 mL, d = 1.00 mm, L = 9.55 m, Pe = 173 (✓ plug flow)

steps.name: Mass Transfer
steps.summary: t_mix = 101.3 s, Da = 1.969 — Transition zone — mixing becoming significant
warnings: Da = 1.97 > 1: mixing may limit conversion. Consider static mixer insert.
assumptions: D = 1e-09 m²/s (typical for small organic molecules in liquid)

steps.name: Heat Transfer
steps.summary: Q_gen = 0.4430 W, Q_rem = 90.0000 W, Da_th = 0.0049 — Thermally safe

steps.name: Back-Pressure Regulator
steps.summary: BPR REQUIRED: 5.0 bar (Gas-liquid system detected — BPR mandatory to maintain gas solubility (Henry's law: C_gas = H·P) and prevent uncontrolled degassing.)
equations: C_{\mathrm{gas}} = H \cdot P_{\mathrm{partial}}\quad\text{(higher BPR} \Rightarrow \text{more dissolved gas)}

steps.name: Process Metrics
steps.summary: STY = 1.6600 mol/(L·h), P = 12.450 mmol/h, ṅ_lim = 0.0333 mmol/min, startup waste = 22.5 mL, IF = 1×

streams.reasoning: From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: Reactants combined in DES solvent. Flow rate derived from total flow (0.5 mL/min) split equally.
From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: Reaction medium. Flow rate derived from total flow (0.5 mL/min) split equally.

proposal.mixing_order_reasoning: From Chemistry Plan: Reagents are added directly to the DES medium. T-mixer provides sufficient initial contact for the viscous DES phase.
post_reactor_steps: Quench with Water: Necessary to stop reaction and facilitate extraction from DES as per Chemistry Plan.
proposal.chemistry_notes: The reaction is a 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition involving the in-situ generation of a nitrile oxide. The use of TBAB/EG DES provides a polar, high-boiling environment. Design is driven by the high temperature (120°C) and the viscosity of the DES. Calculations indicate a laminar flow regime (Re=11) and a Damkohler number (Da=1.97) suggesting that mixing may be a limiting factor; however, the residence time of 15 min is sufficient to match batch conversion. A BPR is mandatory to prevent degassing and maintain phase stability at 120°C.

reasoning_per_field.residence_time_min: Calculation Step 2 estimated 15.0 min based on batch time (15 min) and analogy IF=1x. Analogy 2 used 20 min for a similar [3+2] cycloaddition, supporting this range.
reasoning_per_field.flow_rate_mL_min: Calculation Step 9: Total flow rate set to 0.5 mL/min to achieve the target residence time given the reactor volume.
reasoning_per_field.temperature_C: Maintained at 120°C as per Batch Protocol and Chemistry Plan to ensure rapid nitrile oxide generation and cycloaddition.
reasoning_per_field.concentration_M: Maintained at 0.5 M as per Batch Protocol and Calculation Step 1.
reasoning_per_field.BPR_bar: Calculation Step 8: BPR required (5.0 bar) to prevent degassing and maintain liquid phase at 120°C, as DES components and substrates may generate gas or have vapor pressure concerns.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_material: PFA selected for high temperature (120°C) stability and chemical compatibility with DES, as suggested by Thermal rules.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_ID_mm: Calculation Step 3: 1.0 mm ID used to ensure plug flow (Pe=173) and manage pressure drop (0.032 bar).
reasoning_per_field.reactor_volume_mL: Calculation Step 3: V_R = 7.5 mL (Residence time 15 min * Flow rate 0.5 mL/min).
reasoning_per_field.analogy_comparison: Analogy 1: Similar high-temp cyclization but different chemistry (energetic compound); used for BPR logic. Analogy 2: Chemically similar [3+2] dipolar cycloaddition; used to validate residence time (20 min vs our 15 min). Analogy 3: High-temp thermal cascade; used to validate PFA/Steel tubing for T > 100°C.
